message:
fix(tui): initialise computed views when started via --view flag

When --view lands on a view that lazily computes its data (Tree,
LabelDashboard, FlowMatrix, Sprint, Actionable, Attention), the
constructor leaves the data uninitialised (empty Vec / None).
Normally the toggle functions trigger computation, but --view
bypasses them.

Without this fix, `bvr --view tree` shows "(no dependency tree)"
even when dependencies exist, and other computed views show empty
screens.

Also removes a redundant pre-rebuild tree_cursor clamp in
set_list_filter — it clamped against the old tree length, then
the tree was rebuilt, then the post-rebuild clamp re-clamped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
